changes_made: Renamed k8s_service_mesh_encrypted_traffic_visibility to k8s_service_mesh_tls_traffic_visibility for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's focus on monitoring encrypted communications. However, the naming of 'k8s_service_mesh_encrypted_traffic_visibility' was slightly ambiguous and has been refined for clarity. The functions correctly map to Kubernetes features like NetworkPolicies, service meshes, and Ingress TLS configurations that can provide visibility into encrypted traffic.
naming_improvements: Use 'tls' instead of 'encrypted' for service mesh visibility to specify the type of encryption
title: System Monitoring - System monitoring | visibility of encrypted communications make provisions so th
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4(10) focuses on system monitoring with visibility into encrypted communications. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that network policies and service meshes are configured to monitor encrypted traffic. NetworkPolicies can be used to define and enforce traffic rules, while service meshes like Istio or Linkerd can provide visibility into encrypted traffic. Additionally, ensuring that Ingress controllers have TLS monitoring enabled can help achieve this control.
